isolation.inspectionMode: Read-only source/build/preflight and checked-in handoff inspection plus copying shared evidence into the assigned evidence directory.
constraints: Did not rerun write-heavy mini build, validate, or preflight scripts.
No WeChat runtime, live upload, device, or live backend claims made.
Used shared logs/artifacts plus read-only git proof only.

commandsRun: Read/Grep inspection of README, mission guidance, validation contract, mini source files, repo artifacts, and shared release-preflight evidence.
python3 copy of shared mini-upload-shell evidence into the assigned mission evidence directory plus git-proof.txt creation.

assertions.title: Offline preflight validates repo-owned release config and fails closed on illegal checked-in config
assertions.proofSummary: The copied preflight log passes against the checked-in shell, while the checked-in preflight source rejects bad AppID/runtime/project metadata and tracked local-only WeChat artifacts. Focused release-preflight regressions also cover loopback/illegal-config and tracked-artifact failure paths, so repo-owned release preflight fails closed instead of green-lighting a bad shell.

sourceReferences.note: Checked-in Mini project config preserves AppID, compileType=miniprogram, root=".", and ignoreUploadUnusedFiles=true for import-shell upload readiness.
Release metadata stays semver and exposes the named preflight/upload scaffold commands with miniprogram-ci declared in the checked-in Mini package manifest.
Checked-in runtime stays placeholder-preview on a non-loopback HTTPS target and defines the manual-upload truth boundary plus local-only paths.
Preflight inspects git-tracked local-only paths via git ls-files, requires the shared AppID/runtime/project/script shape, and refuses illegal checked-in config or tracked operator-private artifacts.
Regression tests simulate illegal checked-in AppID/compile/runtime values and tracked private artifacts to confirm the preflight fails closed.

artifactReferences.note: Copied shared preflight output passes the checked-in shell and enumerates the AppID/runtime/project metadata, local-only hygiene, and deferred live runtime/upload scope.
Copied validation log shows the illegal-config and tracked-artifact release-preflight regressions passed.

assertions.title: Release evidence stays source/build-first and hands runtime publish work off through checked-in docs
assertions.proofSummary: The copied build, validation-summary, preflight, and checked-in handoff artifacts all limit the claim to source/build/preflight/manual-upload-shell readiness. They explicitly defer DevTools login, local/private keys, runtime/backend verification, IP allowlisting, audit, and publish work instead of implying live release success.

sourceReferences.note: Shared validation copy defines source-build evidence posture and the manual-upload disclosure that denies real runtime/upload success.
Checked-in handoff source separates truth boundary, validated-now items, deferred operator/runtime/upload steps, local-only secret paths, and the operator checklist.
Preflight output is explicitly tied to the handoff truth boundary and ends by deferring live backend/runtime/upload/publish verification.

artifactReferences.note: Copied build log says the generated artifacts are source/build evidence only and make no real runtime/upload/live-backend claim.
Copied generated summary repeats the disclaimers and stores the same deferred operator/runtime/upload steps and local-only secret paths.
Copied handoff markdown explicitly separates validated shell readiness from deferred operator/runtime/upload work and local-only secret handling.
Copied preflight output reiterates the same truth boundary and deferred operator checklist.

assertions.title: Local/private WeChat artifacts and upload keys stay local and untracked
assertions.proofSummary: Local-only WeChat paths are declared in checked-in runtime config and .gitignore, the preflight inspects the git index to reject tracked/staged operator-private artifacts, and current git proof shows none are tracked. The upload scaffold only accepts runtime-injected secret env vars or local gitignored key files and explicitly says no private key or token material belongs in versioned source.

sourceReferences.note: Repo ignore rules explicitly keep mini/project.private.config.json, mini/config/runtime.local.js, mini/config/runtime.local.json, and mini/upload-secrets/ out of versioned source.
Checked-in runtime config names the local-only runtime override, private DevTools config, upload-secrets directory, and local upload private-key path.
Preflight derives the local-only paths, requires matching .gitignore entries, and fails if git ls-files finds tracked/staged operator-private artifacts.
Upload scaffold expects runtime-injected env vars, keeps keys under the gitignored upload-secrets directory or environment injection only, and states no committed credentials are used.
Tracked private-config/key regressions prove the preflight fails closed if local-only WeChat artifacts are already staged or committed.

artifactReferences.note: Read-only repo proof shows both git status and git ls-files are empty for the local-only WeChat paths checked by this flow.
Copied preflight output keeps the local-only/untracked paths explicit and reports both ignore-rule and tracked-artifact checks as pass.
Copied refusal log demands runtime-injected upload secrets, says keys belong under mini/upload-secrets/ or env vars, and states dry-run never performs a live upload.
Copied ready dry-run log shows the scaffold can proceed with env-injected secret material while still keeping the run dry-run only and local-only.
Copied validation log shows tracked private-artifact regressions and upload-scaffold secret-gate regressions passed.

assertions.title: Upload-shell readiness and deferred runtime/backend validation stay true at the same time
assertions.proofSummary: Product copy in the delivered preview, the checked-in runtime config, the preflight output, the validation summary, and the checked-in handoff doc all tell the same truth: the shell is ready for manual-upload handoff, but real WeChat runtime/device validation, live backend verification, upload, audit, and publish are still deferred.

sourceReferences.note: Shared config sets the preview/runtime disclosures, manual-upload truth boundary, and local-only operator override paths used throughout the product and release surfaces.
Checked-in handoff source keeps validated-now claims separate from deferred operator/runtime/upload steps and renders that split verbatim into markdown.
Surface-state copy says the checked-in shell can pass manual-upload preflight while leaving real backend, runtime, and upload work to later local operator steps.
Delivered Watch copy exposes the placeholder-preview runtime boundary, private DevTools config, local upload secrets, and local-only disclosure through the real shell surface.

artifactReferences.note: Copied preview artifact both disclaims real runtime/upload success and labels the handoff section as manual-upload shell readiness only with deferred live work.
Copied summary artifact stores the same non-runtime disclaimers, truth-boundary label, local-only paths, and deferred operator checklist.
Copied handoff markdown repeats the exact truth boundary, deferred runtime/upload steps, and no-committed-credentials handoff rule.
Copied preflight output says PASS for manual-upload shell readiness while still deferring live backend/runtime/upload/publish verification.
Copied build log keeps the generated proof source/build-only and avoids any live runtime/upload claim.

generatedArtifacts.createdFrom: git status --porcelain and git ls-files --cached read-only repo proof for local-only WeChat paths.

summary: Tested 4 assigned release-preflight/runtime-handoff assertions: 4 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Shared source/build/preflight evidence, product copy, and checked-in handoff artifacts consistently prove manual-upload shell readiness only while keeping live runtime/backend/upload validation deferred and local/private WeChat material untracked.
